user.title: Panasonic TaiSEIA Local
user.description: Sign in to the official EMS account to fetch nicknames / indoor models / ModelType, then multi-select LAN devices to import. The hub entry holds credentials and shared settings.

import_devices.title: Select LAN devices to import

discover.title: Select discovered device
discover.description: Scanned LAN port 57223 and SSDP. Empty list? Keep HA and the module on the same LAN, allow TCP 57223, or use manual IP. ModelType (including UX) only picks the CommandList mapping.

manual.title: Manual setup
manual.description: Enter the TaiSEIA Wi‑Fi module LAN IP (e.g. CZ-T006). Find it via the router or official EMS/App. ModelType (UX, PXGD, …) only affects command mapping.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect. Check the IP and port 57223.
error.no_devices: No locally controllable devices found. Same LAN + open TCP 57223 required, or enter the IP manually. Cloud-only units cannot use this integration.
error.no_selection: Select at least one device, or import later from the integration menu.
error.cloud_api: Cloud API request failed. Try again later.

init.title: Panasonic TaiSEIA options
init.description: Hub: account, LAN tunables, energy cycle (shared). Device: name, ModelType, poll interval, house total, control path (hybrid/local/cloud), control path (hybrid/local/cloud). Without a hub, shared fields appear on the device options.

data.name: Display name
data.energy_cycle_days: Cycle length in days (days cycle only)
data.energy_reset_day: Day of month to reset (1–28, monthly only)
data.control_mode: Control path (hybrid = cloud-first write / LAN-first read)

probe_device.name: Probe device
probe_device.description: Re-probe a configured TaiSEIA module and return its service list (for debugging / App comparison). Prefer the Device picker below.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: Pick the Home Assistant device (recommended). Identifies which LAN module to probe.

entry_id.name: Config entry (advanced)
entry_id.description: Only if you cannot use Device — select this integration's device config entry.

read_service.name: Read TaiSEIA service
read_service.description: Read one raw TaiSEIA service value from a configured device. Use to verify Protocol / App CommandList mapping.

entry_id.name: Config entry (advanced)
entry_id.description: Only if you cannot use Device — select this integration's device config entry.

service.name: Service id
service.description: TaiSEIA service number. Pick a common id or type a custom hex (e.g. 0x15). See probe / diagnostics for the full list.

write_service.name: Write TaiSEIA service
write_service.description: ADVANCED / may change appliance state. Writes a raw TaiSEIA value to a configured device only. Prefer dedicated entities (climate, switches, filter-cleaned button) when available.

entry_id.name: Config entry (advanced)
entry_id.description: Only if you cannot use Device — select this integration's device config entry.

service.name: Service id
service.description: Service to write (e.g. 0x12 filter reset). Prefer dropdown; custom hex allowed.

value.name: Value
value.description: Integer payload to write (often 0=off/reset, 1=on). Confirm against App / diagnostics first.

scan_lan.name: Scan LAN for TaiSEIA modules
scan_lan.description: Discover CZ-T006 / TaiSEIA modules via SSDP and optional /24 TCP 57223 scan. Does not write to devices.

include_subnet_scan.name: Include /24 port scan
include_subnet_scan.description: Also probe every host on the HA subnet for open TCP 57223 (slower, more complete). Turn off for a quick SSDP-only pass.
